title: Make Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Make** (formerly **Integromat**) is a no-code automation / workflow platform — its "scenarios" are visual graphs of modules that fire on triggers and call into HTTP services. This connector — `MakeConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "make"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Make workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Users | `/users` | Current user + list users in an organization |
| Organizations | `/organizations` | List + read organizations the token can see |
| Teams | `/teams` | List + read teams in an organization |
| Scenarios | `/scenarios` | CRUD + start/stop/run |
| Executions | `/executions` | History + drill-down for runs |
| Connections | `/connections` | Third-party authentications a team owns |
| Hooks | `/hooks` | Webhook endpoints (primary inbound trigger surface) |
| Data Stores | `/data-stores` | Persistent K/V stores |
| Templates | `/templates` | Public + team-published scenario templates |
| Devices | `/devices` | Devices registered to a team |

The connector normalises **scenarios** into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces every operation as a standalone `async def` (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::MakeHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Make API token

1. Sign in to https://www.make.com.
2. Open **Profile → API → Add token**.
3. Give the token a label and the scopes you need (Scenarios Read+Write, Hooks, Data Stores, Templates, Devices, etc.).
4. Copy the token value — Make shows it only once.
5. Note your **Zone** from the URL after login: `eu1`, `eu2`, `us1`, `us2`. The connector resolves this to `https://{zone}.make.com/api/v2`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Make**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_token` (secret) | API token from step 4 |
| `zone` (string) | `eu2` / `us1` / … |
| `default_team_id` (number, optional) | Team to use when a call omits one — also used by `sync()` |
| `default_organization_id` (number, optional) | Organization to use when a call omits one |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 60 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Sandbox / proxy override |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the token shape, saves the config, and probes `GET /users/me`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. Network failure → `DEGRADED + AUTHENTICATED` so the user can retry.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /users/me`. The result is classified by `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Make REST API v2 uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry — the token is long-lived and rotated out-of-band at Make → Profile → API.

### Credential model

- `api_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header with the **literal** prefix `Token` — **NOT** `Bearer` (Make-specific gotcha).
- `zone` controls the base URL host: `https://{zone}.make.com/api/v2`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_token`. Saves config. Probes `GET /users/me`. |
| `authorize(auth_code)` | API-key flow — no OAuth exchange. Accepts a rotated token and returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(token_type="Token")`. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /users/me` — minimal probe wrapped in `with_retry(max_retries=2)`. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | Synthetic only — for compat with other connectors. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Token <api_token>     ← Make wire format (NOT Bearer)
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

### Zone → base URL resolution

`helpers/utils.py::build_base_url(zone)` returns `https://{zone}.make.com/api/v2`. Valid zones today: `eu1`, `eu2`, `us1`, `us2`. Future zones (e.g. `asia1`) are accepted permissively — the connector logs a warning at config-time rather than failing fast.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | `Authorization: Token <api_token>` |
| `zone` | string | yes (default `eu2`) | `self.zone` | Selects `https://{zone}.make.com/api/v2` |
| `default_team_id` | number | no | `self.default_team_id` | Default team for `sync()` + helpers |
| `default_organization_id` | number | no | `self.default_organization_id` | Default org for helpers |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no (default 60) | `self.rate_limit_per_min` | Client-side soft cap |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` | Sandbox / proxy override; falls back to `build_base_url(zone)` |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_token", "zone"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `MakeConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::MakeHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_token`; save config; probe `GET /users/me`. Surfaces 401/403 immediately as `INVALID_CREDENTIALS`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state=None) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for the api_key flow — returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(token_type="Token")`. Use it to rotate the in-process token without restarting the connector.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /users/me` with `with_retry(max_retries=2)`. Classifies via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through scenarios for `default_team_id` (or every team the token sees), normalises each into a `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), and calls `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(documents_found/_synced/_failed)`.

children.title: Users · Organizations · Teams
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /users/me`.

### `async list_users(organization_id, page=1, pageSize=50) -> Dict`
`GET /users?organizationId=…`.

### `async list_organizations() -> Dict`
`GET /organizations`.

### `async get_organization(organization_id) -> Dict`
`GET /organizations/{id}`.

### `async list_teams(organization_id) -> Dict`
`GET /teams?organizationId=…`.

### `async get_team(team_id) -> Dict`
`GET /teams/{id}`.

children.title: Scenarios
children.content:
### `async list_scenarios(team_id, page=1, pageSize=50) -> Dict`
`GET /scenarios?teamId=…`.

### `async get_scenario(scenario_id) -> Dict`
`GET /scenarios/{id}`.

### `async create_scenario(team_id, name, blueprint, scheduling=None) -> Dict`
`POST /scenarios` — body: `{teamId, name, blueprint, scheduling?}`.

### `async update_scenario(scenario_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /scenarios/{id}`.

### `async delete_scenario(scenario_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /scenarios/{id}`.

### `async run_scenario(scenario_id, body=None) -> Dict`
`POST /scenarios/{id}/run` — one-shot trigger.

### `async start_scenario(scenario_id) -> Dict`
`POST /scenarios/{id}/start` — enable scheduling.

### `async stop_scenario(scenario_id) -> Dict`
`POST /scenarios/{id}/stop` — disable scheduling.

children.title: Executions · Connections
children.content:
### `async list_executions(scenario_id=None, team_id=None, page=1, pageSize=50) -> Dict`
`GET /executions` — optionally filtered.

### `async get_execution(execution_id) -> Dict`
`GET /executions/{id}`.

### `async list_connections(team_id, page=1, pageSize=50) -> Dict`
`GET /connections?teamId=…`.

### `async get_connection(connection_id) -> Dict`
`GET /connections/{id}`.

children.title: Hooks · Data Stores
children.content:
### `async list_hooks(team_id) -> Dict`
`GET /hooks?teamId=…`.

### `async get_hook(hook_id) -> Dict`
`GET /hooks/{id}`.

### `async create_hook(team_id, name, type_name="webhook") -> Dict`
`POST /hooks` — body: `{teamId, name, typeName}`.

### `async delete_hook(hook_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /hooks/{id}`.

### `async list_data_stores(team_id, page=1, pageSize=50) -> Dict`
`GET /data-stores?teamId=…`.

### `async get_data_store(data_store_id) -> Dict`
`GET /data-stores/{id}`.

children.title: Templates · Devices
children.content:
### `async list_templates(team_id=None, page=1, pageSize=50) -> Dict`
`GET /templates` — public + team-published.

### `async list_devices(team_id) -> Dict`
`GET /devices?teamId=…`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
MakeError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── MakeAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── MakeNotFound                       # 404
├── MakeRateLimitError                 # 429 (honours Retry-After)
└── MakeNetworkError                   # 5xx + transport / timeout
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt + jitter` capped at 8 s, retry up to `_DEFAULT_RETRIES = 3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `MakeNetworkError` |

`helpers/utils.py::with_retry` provides an orchestration-layer retry wrapper for transient JSON-decode flakiness that escapes the HTTP client.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
Network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
Other   → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_token` blank | Re-enter the token in ACP. Make shows the token only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install / health_check | 401 from Make — token revoked or wrong zone | Verify the token is still listed at Make → Profile → API. Verify `zone` matches the host in your Make URL after login (`eu1.make.com` → zone `eu1`). |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` | 403 — token lacks scope | Rotate the token at Make → Profile → API and grant the missing scope (Scenarios, Hooks, etc.). |
| `make.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Make per-minute rate cap) | Client retries with exponential backoff + `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer concurrent requests. |
| `Authorization: Bearer …` in network capture | Wrong scheme | Make wants the literal `Token` prefix, NOT `Bearer`. The client always emits `Token <api_token>`; if you see `Bearer`, you have a code path that bypasses `MakeHTTPClient`. |
| 404 on `/scenarios/{id}` | Wrong scenario id or token's team isn't the scenario's team | Confirm the id via `list_scenarios(team_id=…)`. Make scenarios are scoped to a team — the token must belong to a user in that team. |
| Wrong region — calls hit `eu2` instead of `us1` | Default `zone=eu2` not overridden | Set `zone=us1` (or whichever) in install_fields. The base URL is computed once in `__init__` from `zone` unless `base_url` is also set. |
| `MakeNetworkError` on every call | DNS / TLS / firewall | Confirm outbound HTTPS to `{zone}.make.com` is allowed. If you run behind a proxy, set `base_url` to the proxy. |
